mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid spike in the unemployment rate above 6.5% signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger risk-off positioning among institutional investors, causing immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises above 6.5% within a single month
failure_condition: The signal produces positive or neutral 5-day forward returns in more than 40% of triggered instances across a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in housing starts and rising residential mortgage debt indicates coordinated consumer credit expansion and wealth effects, which jointly drive positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon as corporate revenues accelerate.
candidate_trigger: HOUST and RSAFS both positive month-over-month
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a baseline buy-and-hold strategy by at least 150 basis points over any 3-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained drop in industrial production below 108.0 during a late-cycle expansion phase indicates supply-side bottlenecks resolving into demand weakness, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as earnings revisions turn downward.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below 108.0 for two consecutive months
failure_condition: Industrial production declines during this regime do not correlate with negative equity returns (Rank IC > -0.02 over 3 years).

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 3.0% annualized confirms robust aggregate demand, prompting algorithmic momentum funds to increase equity exposure and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth rate exceeds 3.0% annualized
failure_condition: GDP growth above 3.0% fails to produce positive 5-day forward returns in more than 50% of observations over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in the federal funds rate and the consumer price index signal an aggressive inflation-fighting monetary stance that raises real borrowing costs and compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL both rising month-over-month
failure_condition: The combined rate and inflation signal does not predict negative 60-day forward returns with a statistical significance of p < 0.05 over any 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in total nonfarm payrolls directly improves corporate revenue expectations and consumer spending capacity, mechanically driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days without requiring regime filters.
failure_condition: Positive payroll surprises consistently fail to generate positive 20-day forward returns, with a median return below zero across all quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production surging above 110.0 during a post-shock recovery phase indicates rapid capacity utilization and supply chain normalization, triggering positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as cyclical sector earnings accelerate.
failure_condition: Production spikes above 110.0 during recovery phases do not yield positive 5-day forward returns in at least 60% of cases over a 10-year backtest.
